MV ULTRA ALPHA
BULK CARRIER, KR CLASS, MARSHALL FLAG. BUILT 2015
DWT 63,203MT ON DRAFT 13.30M SSW TPC 62.50MT
GRT 36,203MT / NRT 21,375MT
LOA 199.90M / BREADTH (MOULDED) 32.26M / DEPTH (MOULDED) 18.50M
HOLD CAPACITY 77,784CBM
GRAIN 5H/5H HATCH SIZE NO.1 19.68M X 18.26M / NO.2-5 22.96M X 18.26M
FOUR(4) ELETRO-HYDRAULIC SINGLE DECK CRANE 35T X 30M
FOUR(4) RADIO REMOTE CONTROL TYPE GRAB FITTED 14CBM
BALLAST ABT 14.5KTS ON ABT 25.5MT + 2.5MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1MT LSMGO
LADEN  ABT 14.0KTS ON ABT 26.5MT + 2.5MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1MT LSMGO
IN PORT IDLE ABT 3.5MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1MT LSMGO
WORK ABT 5.5MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1MT LSMGO
FURTHER DETAILS AS PER VESSEL’S DESCRIPTION IN CP--
ECO SPEED/CONSUMPTION:
BALLAST ABT 12.50KNOT / ABT 19 + 2.5MT VLSFO + 0.1MT LSMGO PER DAY
LADEN ABT 11.50KNOT / ABT 20 + 2.5MT VLSFO + 0.1MT LSMGO PER DAY
AND ADD IN ACCORDANCE WITH C/P
IN CASE OF ECO SPEED AND CONSUMPTION AT REDUCED RPM, PROVIDING SAME DOES NOT INTERFERE WITH SAFE OPERATION OF THE VESSEL AND HER MACHINERY, VESSEL ON DAILY BASIS TO INCREASE RPM FOR MINIMUM 1 HOUR DURING WHICH VESSEL TO SAIL AT OPERATIONAL SPEED AND CONSUMPTION.
ALL SPEED/CONSUMPTIONS FIGURES ARE APPROXIMATE PER 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS AND TO BE APPLIED ONLY TO VOYAGES EXCEEDING 48 HOURS TRANSIT TIME FROM SEA PILOT TO SEA PILOT PERFORMED DURING PERIODS OF GOOD WEATHER BEING CONDITIONS UP TO BEAUFORT SCALE 4 / DOUGLAS STATE OF SEA 3 SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT UP TO 3 TO 5 FEET, WITHOUT ADVERSE CURRENT OR SWELL AND EXCLUDING PASSAGE WITH RESTRICTED VISIBILITY AND PASSAGE IN CONGESTED WATERS. SPEED/CONSUMPTION FIGURES ARE NOT TO BE APPLIED TO AND ARE NOT EFFECTIVE IN RESPECT OF ANY PERIODS WHATSOEVER DURING WHICH THE WEATHER CONDITIONS EXCEED BEAUFORT SCALE 4 / DOUGLAS STATE OF SEA 3 SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT UP TO 3 TO 5 FEET WITH ADVERSE CURRENT, OR SWELL OR PASSAGE WITH RESTRICTED VISILBILITY OR IN CONGESTED WATERS . ABOUT IS UNDERSTOOD TO MEAN 0.5KNOTS DOWNWARDS IN THE SPEED AND 5% UPWARDS IN THE CONSUMPTION.
VESSEL HAS LIBERTY TO USE MGO WHEN MANEUVERING IN/OUT OF PORTS OR IN NARROW/SHALLOW WATERS, CANALS OR IN ROUGH SEA, IN RIVER, STARTING UP/TURNING OFF ENGINES, BALLAST/DEBALLAST, CLEANING VESSEL’S HOLD(S).
A. AVERAGE SPEED AND AVERAGE CONSUMPTION TO BE CALCULATED FROM SEA PILOT TO SEA PILOT EXCLUDING PERIODS OF SLOW STEAMING ON CHARTERERS’ ORDER OR FOR REASONS OF SAFETY OR STANDING BY ASSISTING ANOTHER VESSEL IN DISTRESS.
B. FAVORABLE CURRENTS ARE NOT TO BE TAKEN INTO ACCOUNT WHEN EVALUATING VESSEL’S PERFORMANCE RELATED TO SPEED/CONSUMPTION.
C. BUNKER SAVING TO OFFSET ANY TIME LOST AND VICE VERSA.

VESSEL CAN USE HSFO IN MAIN & AUX ENGINES IN ECA AREAS WITH SCRUBBER USE.
ECO SPEED/CONSUMPTION:
BALLAST:  ABT 12.5 KNOTS ON ABT 22.0 MT HSFO  + 0. 1MT LSMGO
LADEN:     ABT 11.5 KNOTS ON ABT 22.0 MT HSFO  + 0.1MT LSMGO
AT PORT:
IDLE
：
ABT 2.5 MTS LSMGO PER DAY
BALLAST/DEBALLAST
：
ABT 3.5 MTS LSMGO  PER DAY
ADDITIONAL  ABT 1.5 MT LSMGO DAILY FOR BOILER CONSUMPTION WITH TEMPERATURE LESS THAN 10 DEGREE CENTIGRADE.
(A) AVERAGE SPEED AND AVERAGE CONSUMPTION TO BE CALCULATED FROM SEA BUOY TO SEA BUOY EXCLUDING PERIODS OF SLOW STEAMING
ON CHARTERERS ORDER OR FOR REASONS OF SAFETY OR STANDING BY OR ASSISTING ANOTHER VESSEL IN DISTRESS.
(B) SPEED AND CONSUMPTION BASIS GOOD WEATHER CONDITIONS OF UP TO BEAUFORT FORCE 4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 (SIGNIFICANT HEIGHT MAX 1.25M)WITH NO NEGATIVE SWELL NO ADVERSE CURRENTS, MINIMUM 24 HOURS STEAMING TIME, MAX SEA SURFACE TEMPERATURE OF 26 DEGREES CELCIUS, EXTRAPOLATION OF “GOOD WEATHER” PERFORMANCE FOR “BAD WE ATHER” PERIODS IS NOT ALLOWED.
(C)VESL MAY BURN SMALL QTYS OF LSMGO WHEN MANEUVERING IN SHALLOW&NARROW WATERS ENTERING/LEAVING PORTS/IN HEAVY WEATHER/CANAL/RIVER/BALLAST/DEBALLAST ETC.
(D) 0.5 KNOT ALLOWANCE  TO BE APPLIED FOR SPEED ASSESSMENT, 5% ALLOWANCE FOR OWNER TO BE APPLIED TO BUNKER CONSUMPTION.
(E) BUNKER SAVING TO OFFSET ANY TIME LOST AND VICE VERSA.
(F) BUNKER SUPPLIED BEING OF ISO8217:2017 (E)  SPECIFICATION, OR 2010, IF 2017 IS NOT AVAILABLE

SPEED / CONS
SPEED AND DAILY BUNKER CONSUMPTION AT SEA BASIS UNDER GOOD WEATHER AND SEA CONDITIONS UPTO BFS4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 WITH
NO ADVERSE CURRENT AND NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL EXISTING AS FLWG.
(IFO 380CST – ISO 8217 2010 – RMG 380)
(MGO – ISO 8217 2010 - DMA MAX 0,1% SULPHUR CONTENT)
EPL SPEEDS
LADEN: ABT 11.50 KN ON ABT 23 MT IFO + ABT 0,1 MT MDO    BALLAST: ABT 12.00 KN ON ABT 23 MT IFO + ABT 0,1 MT MDO
IN PORT IDLE 3,0 MT IFO + 0,2 MDO/LSMGO
IN PORT WORKING 5,0 MT IFO + 0,2 MT MDO/LSMGO BSS CRANE WORKING
ABT MEANS +/- 0.5KNOT IN SPEED AND +/- 5% IN BUNKER CONSUMPTION BOTH IFO AND MDO ARRANGED BY CHARTERERS UNDER THIS CONTRACT TO BE
IN ACCORDANCE WITH RMG 380 RESPECTIVELY DMB SPECIFIED IN ISO 8217 AND ANNEX VI OF MARPOL 73/78 REGULATION 14 WHICH HAS COME INTO FORCE FROM 19TH MAY 2005.
THE VSL ENTITLE TO USE MDO FOR MANEUOUVERING IN NARROW A/O SHALLOW A/O BUSY WATER AREA, CANALS A/O RIVERS, ALSO IN AND OUT OF EACH PORTS,
ENGINE STARTING AND STOPPING, BALLASTING AND DE-BALLASTING OPERATION, AND BURNING OF SLUDGE OIL BY INCINERATOR.
ULO (USED LUBRICATION OIL) OR OTHER WASTE PRODUCTS ARE STRICTLY NOT PERMITTED AND NOT TO BE INCLUDED IN THE BUNKERS SUPPLIES.
GOOD WEATHER CONDITION BASIS EVEN KEEL, 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS ( FROM NOON TO NOON ), NO ADVERSE CURRENT, NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL, BEAUFORT SCALE 4 , DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3
DEFINED AS SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT UP TO 1.25 METERS. ALWAYS EXCLUDING PERIODS DURING
WHICH REDUCTIONS OF SPEED FOR SAFETY, REDUCED VISIBILITY, STEAMING AND/OR MANEUVERING IN CONGESTED WATERS/STRAITS, IN/OUT PORTS ETC.
POSITIVE CURRENT SHALL NOT BE USED IN DIMINISHING VESSEL PERFORMANCE.
COST AND TIME (INCLUDING ANY DEVIATION TIME REQUIRED TO MEET SECA REQUIREMENTS AND/OR
NATIONAL REGULATIONS IN OPERATION IN PORTS TO WHICH VESSEL IS BOUND) INCL BALLAST EXCHANGE TO BE FOR CHARTERERS\' ACCOUNT. ANY SAVING IN CONSUMPTION MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST ANY
REDUCTION IN SPEED, ANY SAVING IN TIME MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST ANY EXCESS CONSUMPTION, ANY SAVING IN FO MUST BE OFF-SET AGAINST INCREASED MDO AND VICE VERSA, AND ANY OVERALL SAVING   ON INDIVIDUAL PASSAGE(S) MUST BE SET OFF AGAINST AN OVERALL LOSS ON OTHER INDIVIDUAL PASSAGE(S).
VESSEL MAY BURN MDO / MGO/ LSMGO IN CASE OF EMERGENCY, DURING MANEUVERING, WHEN
NAVIGATING IN NARROW AND / OR SHALLOW AND/ OR RESTRICTED WATERS AND/ OR IN/ OUT OF PORTS AND/ OR RIVERS AND IF REQUIRED BY LOCAL AND / OR EMISSION LAWS.
ALL ABOVE FIGURES ARE ABOUT, WHICH MEANS HAVING BOTH AN ALLOWANCE OF 0.5KN ON SPEED AND 5% ON BUNKERS OVER-CONSUMPTION, IN OWNERS FAVOUR.
ALL ABOVE FIGURES ARE INCLUSIVE OF AUXILIARY ENGINES.
NO MIXING OF BUNKERS IS ALLOWED AND BUNKER SUPPLY IS ALLOWED UP TO 85% OF INDIVIDUAL TANK CAPACITY. BUNKER TANK RESERVED FOR ECA COMPLIANT FUEL CANNOT ACCOMMODATE IFO 380 CST.
ALL DETAILS ARE ABOUT
